steps.title: Identify the passage's core instruction
steps.content: The passage's central claim is that readers must think of the Aubrey/Maturin series as a single incredibly long work. Everything in the passage—the explanation of the abrupt ending and the comparison to Proust—serves to teach this necessary understanding.

steps.title: Match this understanding to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B matches the passage: understanding the series' structure is helpful for appreciating it.

The other three either claim unsubstantiated critical status, misrepresent structure as a flaw, or overstate the complexity of specific books.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
